stmt: For every p : AtomicResidualState, ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts p ↔ p ≠ ([# p.2], p.2) ∧ ∀ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep p q → ¬ AtomicResidualGraphAccepts q.
summary_delta: This is the exact backward-recursion principle exposed by well-foundedness. It turns termination into a reusable local criterion for rejection, which is a direct bridge toward the missing exact acceptance computation rather than another finiteness repackaging.
priority_rationale: This is the missing local recursion principle for rejection exposed by well-founded residual descent.

stmt: For every `src`, `s`, and `N`, residual acceptance on the bounded slice `{ p : AtomicResidualState | p.2 = s ∧ list_degree p.1 ≤ N ∧ (∀ B ∈ p.1, ∃ C ∈ src, Subformula B C) }` admits an internal finite characterization by backward induction on `list_degree`.
summary_delta: The solved theorem shows that residual steps stay inside this slice and strictly decrease degree, so acceptance should be reducible to a finite downward recursion. That would directly advance the program of replacing external residual-graph reasoning with an input-determined finite internal recognizer.
priority_rationale: An exact backward-inductive characterization on the bounded slice would directly change the summary toward internal recognition.

stmt: ∀ (Γ Δ : List Tp) (A : Tp) (s : String), residualAtomicState Γ A = some (Δ, s) → every finite residual path starting at (Δ, s) has length at most list_degree Δ.
summary_delta: This sharpens reachable-set finiteness to a uniform height bound on residual exploration. That gives a direct, input-determined search cap for exact internal recognition and explains the global shape of the reachable region more clearly than finiteness alone.
priority_rationale: Uniform path-height control is reusable and sharp, but it still mainly supports later recursion theorems.

stmt: For any finite sequence of `n` residual-graph steps from `p` to `q`, if every formula in `p.1` is a subformula of some formula in `src`, then `q.2 = p.2`, every formula in `q.1` is still a subformula of some formula in `src`, and `list_degree q.1 + n ≤ list_degree p.1`.
summary_delta: This is the sharp multi-step abstraction exposed by the solved one-step theorem. It upgrades local descent to a reusable rank statement, yielding uniform path-height control and a degree-layered induction principle for later bounded-machine arguments.
priority_rationale: This is the clean reusable export of strict degree descent, but it still stops short of exact acceptance recursion.

stmt: For every p : AtomicResidualState, there exists q such that Relation.ReflTransGen AtomicResidualGraphStep p q and there is no r with AtomicResidualGraphStep q r.
summary_delta: This promotes well-foundedness from an abstract accessibility fact to an explicit normalization theorem: every residual exploration reaches a terminal state. That gives a concrete endpoint structure for later exact reachability and decision arguments.
priority_rationale: Normalization to terminal states would clarify endpoint structure, but it does not by itself compute acceptance.

stmt: For every Γ : List Tp and s : String, (¬ ∃ q, AtomicResidualGraphStep (Γ, s) q) ↔ ∀ B ∈ Γ, is_atom B.
summary_delta: This sharply identifies the terminal residual states. It explains the obstruction behind bounded-slice overapproximation and separates genuine accepting sinks from nonaccepting dead ends, which is theory-level information the current one-step descent theorem does not yet provide.
priority_rationale: A sharp terminal-state criterion would explain dead ends and support exact recursive acceptance analysis.
